# Rory Webster

First mentioned: **3306-09-18**  
Last mentioned: **3307-01-25**  
Total mentions: **6**

## Related


## Biography

Rory Webster is a former Chief Technician in the Federal Navy who became the central figure in the Starship One sabotage case — one of the most significant criminal trials in Federal history. In October 3306, Webster was charged with mass murder and treason for ordering his engineering team to sabotage Starship One's hyperdrive systems, causing the presidential vessel's destruction in 3301 with hundreds aboard.

During his trial, Webster changed his plea to guilty and claimed he had acted on "orders from the top" — a confession that halted proceedings while the Federal Intelligence Agency took his testimony. In October 3306, he provided evidence that Fleet Admiral Lucas Vincent had directly commanded him to arrange for Starship One to suffer the same fate as the Highliner Antares, with the orders allegedly delivered on a private channel accompanied by threats. Webster received a lenient 20-year sentence in acknowledgment that he performed criminal acts under duress. His testimony exposed high-level conspiracy within the Federal Navy's upper ranks and sent shockwaves through Federation politics, threatening to implicate figures close to President Hudson himself.
